{"detail":"Two successive prior attempts produced zero implementation — only execution result JSON files. This failure pattern is a strong signal the agent is unable to make forward progress on a feature that spans at minimum four distinct subsystems: (1) GraphQL schema generation — deriving Subscription fields from named query declarations, (2) change-feed wiring — detecting entity/link events relevant to a named query's collections and labels, (3) snapshot delivery on subscribe, (4) policy filtering integration (FEAT-029), plus (5) teardown safety and (6) a six-scenario test suite. Each of these concerns has a distinct implementation surface and can be verified independently. The bead acceptance criteria (AC1–AC5) map naturally onto sub-beads. Decomposing reduces cognitive load per execution and makes review failures diagnosable by concern rather than as an undifferentiated miss.","intake_outcome":"too_large_decomposed"}

All 5 acceptance criteria for axon-53c8d772 (FEAT-009 US-077 Subscriptions on schema-declared named queries) are satisfied by code merged to master in commit fc72939 ("feat(axon-graphql): named query subscription fields with initial snapshot (US-077) [axon-53c8d772]").

Implementation:
- crates/axon-graphql/src/dynamic.rs:10413 build_named_query_subscription_field — emits per-named-query Subscription field, delivers initial snapshot, subscribes to BroadcastBroker, re-evaluates on relevant change events filtered by named_query_event_is_relevant.
- crates/axon-graphql/src/dynamic.rs:10581 wires it into build_entity_changed_subscription so each named query yields a Subscription field alongside entityChanged.

Tests (cargo test -p axon-graphql --lib named_query_subscription, all passing):
  AC1 (Subscription field per named query):
    named_query_subscription_fields_appear_in_sdl
  AC2 (Initial snapshot on subscribe):
    named_query_subscription_delivers_initial_snapshot
  AC3 (Updates policy-filtered per FEAT-029):
    named_query_subscription_filters_by_policy
  AC4 (Clean teardown on disconnect):
    named_query_subscription_clean_teardown_on_disconnect
  AC5 (Test scenarios):
    initial snapshot                  — named_query_subscription_delivers_initial_snapshot
    entity-add update                 — named_query_subscription_updates_on_entity_add
    status-change update              — named_query_subscription_updates_on_status_change
    link-add update                   — named_query_subscription_updates_on_link_add
    policy-filter behavior            — named_query_subscription_filters_by_policy
    disconnect cleanup                — named_query_subscription_clean_teardown_on_disconnect

7 of 7 named_query_subscription tests pass. Bead was stuck in needs_human due to two prior ddx try attempts producing zero-diff while the implementation actually landed via fc72939; closing as already_satisfied.
summary: All 5 ACs covered by 7 passing tests in fc72939; closing as already_satisfied
